content.p: Find the interval of convergence for \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle \sum_{ n= 0 }^\infty {{summand}} .

outtro.p: We begin with finding the radius of convergence R. By letting \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} b_n = {{Bn}}, we have that \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} b_{n+1} = {{Bn+1}}. We wish to find the values of x where \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \lim_{n\to\infty} \left| {{Bnp1}} \cdot {{Bnr}} \right| < 1 which occurs precisely when \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} |x - {{a}}| \lim_{n\to\infty} {{Ratio}} < 1 , and by evaluating the limit, this occurs when \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} |x-{{a}}| < 1 or when \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle {{Lpoint}} < x < {{Rpoint}}.
